def split_words(txt):
    '''
    The function processes the input string by first checking for whitespace; if present, it returns a list split on spaces. In the absence of whitespace, it splits on commas. If commas are not found, it returns the number of lowercase letters whose alphabet index is odd, considering ord('a') as 0.
    Examples
    split_words("Hello world!") ➞ ["Hello", "world!"]
    split_words("Hello,world!") ➞ ["Hello", "world!"]
    split_words("abcdef") == 3 
    '''
